title: Amend con_2ce3f2a547: metered synthesis hosts are gated behind an explicit flag, and the guard's host patterns are anchored so a defensive blocklist is not a violation
context: PR #10 adds an opt-in openai-compat/Ollama synthesis provider (self-hosted HTTP, OpenAI chat-completions wire format). Its safety gate refuses known metered API hosts (api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, generativelanguage.googleapis.com, api.mistral.ai, api.cohere.ai, openrouter.ai) unless HUNCH_SYNTH_ALLOW_METERED=1 is set — both in available() (not selected) and as a defense-in-depth throw in run(). Problem discovered on merge: those metered-host STRING LITERALS in the provider's METERED_HOSTS blocklist tripped con_2ce3f2a547's own forbids.patterns (api\.openai\.com / api\.anthropic\.com), so `hunch check --strict` reported the PR as breaking 1 blocking invariant (verified: strictBlockers=1). The defensive blocklist that ENFORCES the invariant read as a VIOLATION of it. This would have failed the repo's own CI Constraint Guard.
decision: con_2ce3f2a547's spirit is 'never SILENTLY bill', not 'no metered host is ever reachable': a metered endpoint is allowed only as a deliberate, named act via HUNCH_SYNTH_ALLOW_METERED=1. To keep the guard precise, anchor its two host patterns from `api\.openai\.com` to `[/.]api\.openai\.com` (and the anthropic one likewise): a bare blocklist entry "api.openai.com" (preceded by a quote) now COMPLIES, while a real call `https://api.openai.com/...` (preceded by /) and a subdomain `eu.api.openai.com` (preceded by .) are still CAUGHT. The dep tier (@anthropic-ai/sdk, openai) and the `new OpenAI(` / `new Anthropic(` patterns are unchanged. Applied to BOTH the public .hunch and the private overlay copy (per dec_57e3dcca52: the overlay copy wins recs() id-dedupe, so a public-only edit would silently not take effect on maintainer machines).
consequences: `hunch check --strict` and the CI Constraint Guard pass on PR #10 (verified exit 0, 9 changed files, no invariant hits).
A future raw fetch/baseURL to api.openai.com / api.anthropic.com in src/synthesis/** is still blocked by the lint tier; the SDK-import and constructor paths are unchanged.
Metered LLM synthesis is possible but only via an explicit, named opt-in (HUNCH_SYNTH_ALLOW_METERED=1); local/self-hosted/LAN endpoints are unaffected.
The overlay constraint copy must be kept in sync with the public copy for any future matcher change (dec_57e3dcca52).
alternatives_rejected: Remove the api.*.com patterns entirely and rely on the dep tier — rejected: it drops the only lint-tier catch for a RAW fetch to a metered host that imports no SDK, which is exactly the transport this PR introduces.

Anchor with `//` only (require a URL scheme) — rejected: it misses subdomain URLs like eu.api.openai.com that the original unanchored pattern caught and that the runtime gate's suffix-match still catches; [/.] preserves subdomain coverage.

Lower con_2ce3f2a547 severity or firmness so the touch is advisory — rejected (echoes dec_57e3dcca52): precision, not permissiveness; weakening the gate would let a genuine re-introduction through for humans and agents alike.
Obfuscate the blocklist strings so they dodge the matcher — rejected: hides intent and defeats the point of a readable safety list.
